source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Classic BBQ-side potato salad: creamy, slightly tangy, with crunchy add-ins.
Designed to be made ahead and served chilled; improves after resting.
Supports Southern-style options (sweet relish, mustard-forward) and dill/herb variation.

yield.notes: Serves 8–12 as a side depending on the spread.

ingredients.label: Yukon Gold or red potatoes
ingredients.notes: Waxier potatoes hold shape. Peel if desired (optional). Cut into 3/4-inch chunks.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt (for boiling water)
ingredients.notes: Season the water well; adjust to taste.

ingredients.label: Large eggs
ingredients.notes: Hard-boiled; chop or slice for garnish.

ingredients.label: Mayonnaise
ingredients.notes: Use a good mayo. For lighter, sub part with Greek yogurt or sour cream.

ingredients.label: Yellow mustard
ingredients.notes: Classic BBQ potato salad flavor. Dijon works for a sharper version.

ingredients.label: Apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: Adds tang; can swap with pickle juice for extra bite.

ingredients.label: Sugar
ingredients.notes: Optional—balances tang (especially if using vinegar-forward BBQ mains).

ingredients.label: Red onion, finely diced
ingredients.notes: Or use sweet onion. Rinse diced onion under cold water to mellow if desired.

ingredients.label: Sweet pickle relish
ingredients.notes: Classic Southern style. Substitute diced dill pickles for less sweet.

ingredients.label: Pickle juice (optional)
ingredients.notes: Use in place of some vinegar for extra punch.

ingredients.label: Celery seed
ingredients.notes: Old-school potato salad note; optional but recommended if you have it.

ingredients.label: Paprika
ingredients.notes: For flavor + garnish. Smoked paprika is nice with BBQ.

ingredients.label: Fresh dill, chopped (optional herb version)
ingredients.notes: Swap in for a dill-forward version with dill pickles and Dijon.

steps.title: Hard-boil the eggs
steps.body: Place eggs in a saucepan and cover with cold water by 1 inch. Bring to a boil, cover, and turn off heat. Let sit 10–12 minutes. Transfer to an ice bath, cool, peel, and chop.

steps.title: Boil the potatoes
steps.body: Add cut potatoes to a large pot and cover with cold water by 1 inch. Salt the water. Bring to a gentle boil and cook until potatoes are fork-tender but not falling apart, about 10–14 minutes depending on size. Drain well.

steps.title: Cool and season the potatoes
steps.body: Let potatoes steam-dry in the colander 2–3 minutes, then spread on a sheet pan to cool 10 minutes. While still warm, sprinkle lightly with a pinch of salt and (optional) a splash of vinegar or pickle juice for better flavor absorption.

steps.title: Mix the dressing
steps.body: In a large bowl, whisk mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar (or pickle juice), sugar (optional), celery seed (optional), garlic powder (optional), black pepper, and paprika. Taste and adjust: more mustard for bite, more vinegar for tang, a pinch of salt if needed.

steps.title: Fold everything together
steps.body: Add cooled potatoes to the bowl. Add celery, onion, relish/pickles if using, and most of the chopped eggs (reserve a little for garnish). Fold gently until coated. Don’t overmix or the potatoes will get gummy.

steps.title: Chill for best flavor
steps.body: Cover and refrigerate at least 1 hour (preferably 3–4 hours) to let flavors meld. Before serving, stir gently and adjust seasoning. Garnish with reserved egg, paprika, and green onions if desired.

refrigerate.instructions: Store covered in the refrigerator. Keep cold at service; return to fridge promptly.

storage.notes: Food safety: avoid leaving out >2 hours (or >1 hour if very hot outside).

serveModes.label: BBQ Plate Side
serveModes.notes: Classic side for brisket, ribs, pulled pork, smoked chicken, and sausage.

serveModes.label: Potluck Bowl
serveModes.notes: Make the night before for best flavor. Keep chilled on ice at outdoor events.

notes: Increase yellow mustard to 3 tbsp; add 1/2 tsp turmeric for color (optional).
Use sweet relish + a pinch of sugar for classic sweet-tang balance.
Swap sweet relish for diced dill pickles; use Dijon instead of yellow mustard.
Add fresh dill and a splash of pickle juice; reduce sugar or omit.
Replace 1/3–1/2 of the mayo with Greek yogurt or sour cream.
Add extra vinegar/pickle juice to maintain tang.
Do not overboil: potatoes should be fork-tender but hold shape.
Fold gently; cool potatoes before mixing to avoid gummy texture.
